title: Buttery, Cheese-Pull Russian Cheese Pilaf
description: A cozy, comforting pilaf originating from Russia, featuring saffron and melted cheese, suitable for a lacto-vegetarian diet, and perfect for a cold evening. This dish is cooked in a comal, a traditional Russian cooking vessel.
instructions: Heat a comal or heavy skillet over medium heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of butter and let it melt, then dust the comal with flour, swirling the flour to coat the surface.
Add the onion and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are lightly browned.
Add the rice and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes, coating the rice with the butter mixture.
Add the vegetable broth, milk, salt, and black pepper, then stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 18 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in the saffron threads, white cheddar cheese, and cheddar cheese until the cheese is melted and the pilaf is creamy.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Note: This dish is best made in a comal or a heavy skillet, as it allows for even heat distribution and prevents the rice from burning.
If using a regular skillet, adjust the heat to prevent burning, and stir the pilaf frequently to ensure it cooks evenly.
